title: Documentation — credential storage architecture and local setup
description: Add accurate doc comments to CredentialsStore (storage contract: which keys go to UserDefaults vs Keychain, injectable test initialiser usage), KeychainHelper (upsert semantics, key namespace), and APIClient (CF header injection, path construction). Verify CLAUDE.md already covers the xcodegen + xcodebuild workflow; add a brief note if anything is missing for future developers onboarding to the project.
acceptance_criteria: CredentialsStore has a doc comment describing which properties persist to UserDefaults vs Keychain, default values, and how to use the test initialiser
KeychainHelper has a doc comment describing the upsert behaviour and key namespace
APIClient has a doc comment describing CF header injection and how path is constructed from baseURL
CLAUDE.md accurately reflects the xcodegen + simulator test command for this project (update if stale)

handoff_summary.completed: Doc comments added to all three Common/ files covering storage contract, upsert semantics, and CF header injection. CLAUDE.md Project shape corrected from iOS 17+ to iOS 26+ to match the deployment target stated in UI conventions.
handoff_summary.remaining: Nothing. All acceptance criteria are satisfied.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Changes are pure doc comments and a one-word version string correction; no runtime behavior altered.
handoff_summary.next_action: No further action required. This bead is complete.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Doc comment changes are additive and do not touch any code paths shared with sibling beads.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: CLAUDE.md Project shape section said iOS 17+ but UI conventions already mandated iOS 26 as the deployment target; corrected to iOS 26+ for consistency. No other design decisions required.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: SourceKit shows pre-existing cross-file symbol resolution errors in isolation (CredentialsStore.swift referencing KeychainHelper, APIClient.swift referencing CredentialsStore/Source/HealthResponse). These are not introduced by this bead and resolve when xcodegen + xcodebuild compiles the full project.

conflict_risks: None. Doc comment changes are additive and do not touch any code paths shared with sibling beads.

execution_history.summary: Added doc comments to CredentialsStore, KeychainHelper, and APIClient; corrected stale iOS version in CLAUDE.md

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.

worker_prompt_context.conflict_risks:
AggregatorApp.xcodeproj is gitignored and must be regenerated with `xcodegen generate` by any sibling bead or after merge to main before building. Beads that add new Swift source files must update project.yml sources paths or place files under the existing AggregatorApp/ or AggregatorAppTests/ directories.
None. These are pure value-type models with no state; downstream beads depending on Source or HealthResponse can import them without risk.
None. These three view files are owned exclusively by this bead; SourcesView.swift and SettingsView.swift (owned by sibling beads) each independently wrap themselves in their own NavigationStack.
None. KeychainHelper is standalone; CredentialsStore injects it via closures so no interface change.
None — files are complete and build is clean.
None. Tests only read from AggregatorApp module types (CredentialsStore, Source) — no shared mutable state.
None. APIClient.swift is a new file with no overlap with other in-progress beads.
None. SettingsView.swift is self-contained and only reads from CredentialsStore and APIClient, both stabilized by dependency beads B-73a6c60f and B-bb90df5b.
No conflicts expected. SourcesView.swift is the sole file changed; it imports only types defined in prior beads (CredentialsStore, APIClient, Source).
